A test case to verify the logical negation and strict equality in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the logical negation of a boolean value. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the expression `!true === false` evaluates to `true`. The expression `!true` results in `false`, and the comparison `false === false` is `true`, so the assertion passes. This code provides narrow functionality, serving as a basic test to confirm the behavior of logical negation and equality operations in JavaScript.
